title: Laundry Basket Organizer Tower
summary: A tall plywood tower for four stacked laundry baskets, built as two mirrored sides with 1x2 wood cleats — a fixed-size alternative to this catalog's other basket organizers.
description:
This tower takes a different structural approach than this catalog's other basket organizers: instead of the laundry-basket-dresser's metal angle rails or the formula-based frames sized off your own baskets, this is a single tall plywood case with 1x2 wood cleats screwed to two MIRRORED side panels, stacking four baskets vertically in one lengthwise column rather than three side by side.

**The two sides must be built as true mirrors of each other, not two identical panels** — the source calls this out directly: build two the same and you've built a shell for two dresser-style units, not this tower. Mark the less attractive face and edge of your plywood as the "back" side on both panels before laying out cleat positions, and always make at least two marks per cleat row so each one goes on square.

Rip the plywood into the back, sides, and top/bottom panels first, accounting for saw kerf between cuts — the source notes you genuinely cannot just mark every cut line at once and start cutting, since each cut consumes a small amount of material the layout has to account for. **Measure the actual baskets you're buying before committing to the case width** — this design is sized around a specific 16" x 24" basket, and a different basket size changes every interior dimension.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood sheet into all case panels.

materials.name: Self-tapping star bit screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"
Laundry baskets, 16 in x 24 in
materials.note: Measure and confirm fit before building the case.

cutList.note: Built as true mirrors of each other, not identical panels.

steps.title: Rip the plywood panels
steps.body: Starting at one end of the plywood sheet, measure and cut the back panel, then the two side panels, then rip the remaining strip into the top and bottom panels. Account for saw kerf between each cut rather than marking all cuts at once.

steps.title: Mark the back edges
steps.body: Identify the less attractive face and edge of each side panel and mark it as the back side, 3/4" in from the edge, on both panels.

steps.title: Lay out and attach the cleats
steps.body: Mark cleat positions down each side panel, with at least two marks per row so each cleat goes on square. Build the two side panels as true mirrors of each other — building them identical instead creates two dresser-style units, not this tower. Apply glue to the back edge of each cleat and nail down with 1-1/4" brad nails.

steps.title: Assemble the case
steps.body: Attach the back panel and the two mirrored side panels together with screws and glue.
materials: Self-tapping star bit screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Add the top and bottom and finish
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom panels, keeping outside edges flush, with glue. Sand and prime and paint. Slide in the four laundry baskets to confirm fit.

unresolvedImages.alt: Tall plywood tower holding four stacked laundry baskets
